name: Spaghetti Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Brown the Italian sausage with onion and drain any excess fat.
2. Add the remaining ingredients (tomato paste, tomato sauce, water, garlic, bay leaves, sugar, dried basil, dried oregano, fresh parsley, and salt) to a large pot or Dutch oven.
3. Stir all the ingredients together until well combined.
4. Bring the mixture to a simmer over medium-low heat, stirring occasionally.
5. Cover partially and continue simmering for 2 hours, stirring occasionally, until the flavors are well developed.
6. Alternatively, you can simmer the sauce in a crockpot on low for the same duration.
7. Once finished cooking, allow the sauce to cool slightly before transferring it to separate containers.
8. Store the sauce in the refrigerator or freezer for later use with Cannelloni and spaghetti dishes.
9. When ready to serve, thaw the sauce if frozen and heat thoroughly until warmed through.
10. Serve the warm sauce over 8 ounces of cooked spaghetti, accompanied by a tossed salad, fresh broccoli or green beans, and Italian bread.
